name: n-acetylcysteine (nac)
mechanism: Replenishes glutathione by serving as a cysteine precursor; also has direct reducing activity and breaks disulfide bonds in mucus.
contraindications: activated charcoal can reduce absorption in overdose settings.
summary: Replenishes glutathione by serving as a cysteine precursor; also has direct reducing activity and breaks disulfide bonds in mucus.
description: Replenishes glutathione by serving as a cysteine precursor; also has direct reducing activity and breaks disulfide bonds in mucus.
